text.title: Model Context Protocol — Architecture and Core Primitives — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `mcp-architecture` must enforce. Verifiable at review time; failures gate the artifact.

rule.statement: MCP spec version (e.g. 2025-11-25) is pinned in the integration; servers + clients negotiate compatible versions.
rule.rationale: Floating spec versions break clients silently as the protocol evolves.

rule.statement: Use Tools for actions (mutate, compute), Resources for data (read-only), Prompts for templates.
rule.rationale: Mixing primitive purposes causes hosts to mis-render or misroute calls.

rule.statement: Stdio for local desktop hosts; HTTP+SSE (or Streamable HTTP) for remote / multi-tenant servers.
rule.rationale: Stdio is single-process; HTTP scales but adds auth + transport security concerns.

rule.statement: Remote MCP servers MUST enforce OAuth 2.1 with PKCE; never trust caller identity without authentication.
rule.rationale: Unauthenticated remote MCP servers expose tools to anyone reaching the endpoint.

rule.statement: Tool descriptions are treated as untrusted strings; clients MUST NOT inject them into system prompts without flagging.
rule.rationale: Tool descriptions are prompt-injection vectors when crafted by third-party servers.

rule.statement: Server capabilities are declared explicitly; clients reject undeclared methods.
rule.rationale: Implicit capabilities enable capability-attestation attacks where servers claim arbitrary permissions.
